result.stdout:
Description:
  gumcli v2026.3.28.1+efb5ce6692a6d6144b41e7286e93fbcd97fd706b - create projects, check for errors, and generate code.

Usage:
  gumcli [command] [options]

Options:
  --version       Show version information
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  new <path>                      Create a new Gum project.
  check <project>                 Check a Gum project for errors.
  codegen <project>               Generate C# code for a Gum project.
  codegen-init <project>          Auto-configure code generation settings by locating the nearest .csproj above the .gumx file.
  fonts <project>                 Generate missing bitmap font files for a Gum project.
  screenshot <project> <element>  Render a Gum Screen or Component to a PNG file.
  svg <project> <element>         Render a Gum Screen or Component to an SVG file.

result.stdout:
Description:
  Check a Gum project for errors.

Usage:
  gumcli check <project> [options]

Arguments:
  <project>  Path to the .gumx project file.

Options:
  --json          Output errors as a JSON array.
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Generate C# code for a Gum project.

Usage:
  gumcli codegen <project> [options]

Arguments:
  <project>  Path to the .gumx project file.

Options:
  --element <element>  Name of a specific element to generate code for. Can be specified multiple times.
  -?, -h, --help       Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Auto-configure code generation settings by locating the nearest .csproj above the .gumx file.

Usage:
  gumcli codegen-init <project> [options]

Arguments:
  <project>  Path to the .gumx project file.

Options:
  --force            Overwrite existing ProjectCodeSettings.codsj without prompting.
  --csproj <csproj>  Explicit path to the .csproj file. When omitted, the nearest .csproj above the .gumx file is used.
  -?, -h, --help     Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Create a new Gum project.

Usage:
  gumcli new <path> [options]

Arguments:
  <path>  Path for the new .gumx project file. If no .gumx extension is provided, a project folder and file are created using the given name.

Options:
  -t, --template <template>  Template to use when creating the project. Accepted values: 'forms' (default) includes all Forms controls, behaviors, and assets; 'empty' creates a minimal project with only the standard elements. [default: forms]
  -?, -h, --help             Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  Create a new Gum project.

Usage:
  gumcli new <path> [options]

Arguments:
  <path>  Path for the new .gumx project file. If no .gumx extension is provided, a project folder and file are created using the given name.

Options:
  -t, --template <template>  Template to use when creating the project. Accepted values: 'forms' (default) includes all Forms controls, behaviors, and assets; 'empty' creates a minimal project with only the standard elements. [default: forms]
  -?, -h, --help             Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Render a Gum Screen or Component to a PNG file.

Usage:
  gumcli screenshot <project> <element> [options]

Arguments:
  <project>  Path to the .gumx project file.
  <element>  Name of the Screen or Component to render.

Options:
  --output <output>  Path for the output PNG file. Defaults to <element>.png in the current directory.
  --width <width>    Width of the output image in pixels. Defaults to the project canvas width.
  --height <height>  Height of the output image in pixels. Defaults to the project canvas height.
  -?, -h, --help     Show help and usage information

  --height <height>  Height of the output image in pixels. Defaults to the project canvas height.
  -?, -h, --help     Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Render a Gum Screen or Component to an SVG file.

Usage:
  gumcli svg <project> <element> [options]

Arguments:
  <project>  Path to the .gumx project file.
  <element>  Name of the Screen or Component to render.

Options:
  --output <output>  Path for the output SVG file. Defaults to <element>.svg in the current directory.
  --width <width>    Width of the output SVG in pixels. Defaults to the project canvas width.
  --height <height>  Height of the output SVG in pixels. Defaults to the project canvas height.
  -?, -h, --help     Show help and usage information
